findings.description: The 99.9% latency reduction claim is implausible and inconsistent with the sub-second latency claim in the Performance section.
findings.evidence: "The in-memory delivery cache reduces end-to-end latency by 99.9% compared to unbuffered writes." If sub-second is the cached latency, uncached would be ~1000 seconds.
findings.suggestion: Replace with a measured, defensible figure.

findings.description: The sub-second end-to-end latency claim is directly contradicted by the 5-minute polling interval for batch completion in Stage 3.
findings.evidence: "Polling for batch completion uses a 5-minute interval." vs "the pipeline maintains sub-second end-to-end latency for 95th-percentile events." A 5-minute poll means batch completion latency is up to 5 minutes.
findings.suggestion: Reconcile: either reduce polling interval to seconds or scope the latency claim to exclude batch completion acknowledgment.
